title: Naming Rules on ODBC
text: Naming Rules on ODBC The format for index names depends on the value of the key syntax connector option. If the data source is not a DOS file, the format is: I_ TableName _ IndexNumber TableName —name of the Uniface entity IndexNumber —number of the index The following rules apply: Letters, numbers, dollar signs ( $ ), and underscores ( _ ) are allowed, but the first character must be a letter. Note: Uniface accepts only uppercase names in the application model definition. If the data source is a DOS file, the format of the index name is indx nnnn , where nnnn is a incremental value starting at ‘0001’. You may not use any of the DBMS reserved words for the names of entities or fields. These reserved words are listed in the DBMS documentation. Table names must also comply with Uniface rules. The same rules apply to field names as for tables. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
